diagrams.title: Two's Complement Number Representation
diagrams.description: Shows how signed integers are represented using two's complement notation, demonstrating the conversion from positive to negative numbers and the bit patterns for values from -4 to +3 using 4 bits.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how two's complement allows simple binary arithmetic circuits to perform subtraction using the same hardware as addition, and demonstrates the asymmetric range of representable values.

diagrams.title: Binary Arithmetic Operations
diagrams.description: Visualizes step-by-step binary addition and subtraction operations, showing bit-by-bit computation with carry/borrow propagation, and demonstrates overflow detection in signed arithmetic.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how CPU arithmetic logic units (ALUs) perform integer operations in binary, including the mechanics of carry propagation and when overflow conditions occur.

diagrams.title: IEEE 754 Floating-Point Format
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a 32-bit floating-point number with sign bit, exponent field, and mantissa (fraction) field, along with special values (denormalized, infinity, NaN) and the concept of normalized representation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how real numbers are approximated in computers using the IEEE 754 standard, showing the trade-offs between range and precision, and why floating-point arithmetic requires special handling.
